# Figure description: Figure 1.jpg

**Generated by**: claude-sonnet-4-6

---

## Figure Type
This is a **project location plan and exploration index map** (Figure 1), combining a topographic base map with soil geochemical sampling results (gold-in-soil anomalism) overlaid on the tenure boundary. It functions as both an index map and a preliminary geochemical results map.

---

## Coverage and Subject
- **Exploration licence:** E20/742 (Enterprise Metals Limited)
- **Location:** Murchison region, Western Australia, near the town of **Coe**
- **Map projection:** MGA94 Zone 50
- **Scale:** 1:100,000
- **Coordinate range:** Eastings ~575,000–990,000 mE; Northings ~6,975,000–6,990,000 mN (GDA94/MGA94)
- **Date drawn:** 4 October 2018, Author: MK
- **Named localities/features:** Wattagee Hill (463 m), 9 Mile Well, No. 2 Bore, No. 3 Well, No. 4 Well, Milly Bore, Milly Well, Railway Bore, Emily Well, Cuddingwarra Mine, abandoned racecourse
- **Road:** Beringarra-Coe Road (running NNW–SSE through the western portion)
- **Drainage:** Emily Creek and associated blue drainage lines

---

## Key Content

### Tenure
The E20/742 exploration licence boundary is delineated by a **bold black irregular polygon**, covering roughly 15–20 km N–S and 10–15 km E–W in the central-to-eastern portion of the map. There is a notable rectangular notch excised from the northern boundary (suggesting a pre-existing tenement or exclusion zone).

### Soil Geochemical Sampling — Au (ppb)
Soil sampling results are plotted as coloured dots using the following legend categories:
| Symbol | Au concentration |
|--------|-----------------|
| Small open circle | 0–5 ppb |
| Small solid blue dot | 6–10 ppb |
| Small solid yellow dot | 11–15 ppb |
| Solid red dot | 16–20 ppb |
| Solid pink/magenta dot | >20 ppb |

The sampling appears concentrated in a **cluster near the centre-west of the tenement**, roughly at coordinates ~580,000 mE / 6,979,000 mN, coinciding with a hatched/gridded pattern (suggesting a mine or pit area near Cuddingwarra Mine). The dots visible in this cluster appear predominantly in the lower concentration ranges (0–10 ppb), though the resolution suggests some elevated values may be present.

### Geological/Structural Context
- **Red curvilinear lines** represent geological contacts or structural features (likely lithological boundaries or fault traces), consistent with Yilgarn/Murchison greenstone belt geology
- **Green shading** represents areas of likely greenstone or mafic/ultramafic lithology (consistent with greenstone belt outcrop)
- **Blue lines** are drainage/watercourses
- Several **historic mine workings symbols** (crossed-pick icons) are present within and near the tenement, indicating prior mining activity

### Inset Location Map
A small inset in the lower-left shows the tenement location within Western Australia, with reference towns: Port Hedland, Geraldton, Kalgoorlie, Perth, Esperance. The tenement falls in the **Coe** region (Murchison Goldfield), confirmed by the township index showing Coe, Reedy, Murgoo, Nookawarra, Madoonga, Meekatharr, Austin, Wynyangoo.

## Research Value

This figure establishes the **spatial context and preliminary gold-in-soil geochemical footprint** of Enterprise Metals' E20/742 tenement near Coe in the Murchison Goldfield, a historically significant gold-producing terrane within the Yilgarn Craton. It confirms the tenement's proximity to the **Cuddingwarra Mine** and multiple historic workings, indicating the area has proven gold mineralisation, and allows a researcher to assess whether the soil geochemical anomalism is spatially associated with mapped structural features (the red lithological/fault contacts) or drainage dispersion pathways. For an AI research assistant, this figure answers *"where is this project located and is there a surface geochemical gold signature?"* — critical first-pass information for evaluating the exploration model and targeting strategy within the tenement.
